preamble: Lateral palatalization involves the shift from the alveolar lateral [l] to the palatal lateral [ʎ]. This change is extremely common when /l/ is followed by the palatal glide [j] or high front vowels. It is a defining historical development in Italian, Spanish, and several Slavic languages.

examples.note: The palatal lateral /ʎ/ (written 'lh') is a core phoneme in Portuguese.

sources: Ladefoged, P., & Maddieson, I. (1996). The Sounds of the World's Languages.
Schenker, A. M. (1995). The Dawn of Slavic.
Alkire, T., & Rosen, C. (2010). Romance Philology.
